title: Logd Output & API Design: Living Critique
summary: A living knowledge base capturing the design critique, theming architecture analysis, and futuristic API vision for logd's output pipeline. Documents the current state of HtmlEncoder, AnsiEncoder, MarkdownEncoder, the Handler/Sink/Encoder/Formatter composition model, WrappingStrategy, and theme ownership. Captures both what is correct and what is immature, with a forward-looking, non-breaking roadmap philosophy. Intended to grow as logd matures through real user interactions. Current design tensions: Formatter/Encoder naming ambiguity, WrappingStrategy as invisible user tax, theme split across pipeline stages, missing Beginner Path (LogOutput facade), absent LogSurface concept in LogTheme, and sink lifecycle ownership confusion.
